Selection Of Provisional Director
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Continued To Nov-02-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Lowenthal of Styer & Lowenthal LLP telephone number 415-421-3400 for Plaintiff Benjamin Eisler.
Robert Infelise and Christopher Gribble of Cox, Castle & Nicholson LLP telephone number 415-262-5125 for Defendant Allen Orwitz.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling and put the matter over for one week. If the Parties are unable to select someone who does not have any connections to either of their Clients by the end of the day 4:00 p.m. November 1, 2016, the Court will choose someone from the appartment management business. Selection Of Provisional Director is continued to November 2, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion For Appointment Of Provisional Director Pursuant To Section 308 Of The California Corporations Code
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert W. Biederman of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415.421.3400) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER BENJAMIN EISLER's Motion For Appointment Of Provisional Director Pursuant To Section 308 Of The California Corporations Code is GRANTED. Section 2.2 of BACO Realty Corporation's by-laws mandate that there be three directors and BACO currently only has two. The following evidence shows that there is a deadlock "so that [BACO's] business can no longer be conducted to advantage or so that there is danger that its property and business will be impaired or lost." (Corporations Code 308.) At the directors meeting of October 21, 2015, the directors could not agree on the appointment of a third director, whether the affiliation between BACO and ISM Management Co. LLC would continue, implementation of clear rules regarding management responsibilities, and implementation of a merit-based bonus program. (Eisler Declaration paragraphs 15-16.) The evidence further shows that Mr. Orwitz has refused to sign routine amendments to operating agreements to allow for refinancing of the single asset entities and the parties' deadlock has adversely affected the ability to sell properties. (Eisler Declaration paragraphs 17-19.) Mr. Orwitz's e-mails, which are attached to Mr. Eisler's declaration, show a strained relationship and inability of the directors to work together. There is no basis to continue this motion to allow for further discovery. The discovery that is the basis of the continuance request is only tangentially related to the deadlock issue. The motion is continued to October 25, 2016, to allow the parties to meet and confer regarding the selection of the provisional director. If the parties cannot agree, no later than October 18, 2016 they can each nominate up to two individuals for appointment that meet the qualification requirements of Corporations Code 308(c) and who are not currently or formerly affiliated with only one of the two current directors or have a reason to favor one over the other. The parties' nomination papers shall include the resume or cv of each nominee. The court will select the provisional director at the October 25, 2016 hearing. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
